title: Jacquemontia rufa
intro: Jacquemontia rufa, also known as red manjack or red climber, is a flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Jacquemontia rufa has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Jacquemontia rufa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Jacquemontia rufa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections, sore throats, and coughs.
taxonomy: Jacquemontia rufa is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Convolvulaceae and the genus Jacquemontia. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Jacquemontia rufa is a type of vine, characterized by its climbing habit and twining stems. The species is further classified as Jacquemontia rufa within the plant kingdom, with the scientific classification being Jacquemontia rufa (Bureau) Verdc. The classification places Jacquemontia rufa within the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms.
